verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically cytosolic and intramitochondrial (TIM23 complex component). However, HSP70 chaperones undergo well-documented cell-state-induced surface externalization during cellular stress (heat shock, hypoxia, ER stress, oxidative stress, nutrient deprivation) and oncogenic transformation. Cancer cells display HSP70 on the outer leaflet as an immunogenic ecto-antigen and damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP). Additionally, HSP70 translocates to the PM during apoptosis and immunogenic cell death. These non-baseline surface pools, driven by stress and cellular-state changes, qualify the protein as contextual surface accessible.
